reason: The statement applies because in this text fragment, Nebuchadnezzar, the king of Babylon, set up a golden image and commanded all to worship it. However, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who were Jews serving in Babylon, refused to worship the image and remained faithful to their own God. This defiance against the king's decree is akin to a prophetic stance of standing against idolatry and can be seen as a form of forewarning by Yahweh.
